# Book references & cited sources for the research follow-ups
*Auto-generated cross-reference.* For every open research follow-up in [`follow_ups.html`](./follow_ups.html) (the 72 questions Christine Cotton raises across her 18 interviews), this document gives two things: (1) **where she discusses it** in her English book — *The Trial Was Almost Perfect* (20 October 2024, 213 pp) — with verbatim quotes, and (2) the **underlying source documents she cites** there, resolved from the book's footnotes to the actual publisher/URL (FDA briefings, the C4591001 protocol, EMA EPARs, the Moderna SEC 10-K, HAS/OPECST reports, etc.). Quotes are verbatim from [`archive/documents/Book_The_Trial_was_Almost_Perfect_EN.md`](../archive/documents/Book_The_Trial_was_Almost_Perfect_EN.md); page numbers are the book's own printed markers.

**58 of 72** follow-ups are addressed in the book (165 passages cited), resolving to **160 underlying source documents** across 55 follow-ups. Items marked *Not found in book* are follow-ups the book does not cover.

---

## Le désastre sanitaire ne fait que commencer I Christine Cotton
<a id="wmdftibk-ek"></a>
*Cercle Aristote - Pierre Yves Rougeyron · [watch ▶](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WmDFTibK-Ek) · [brief](./brief.html?id=WmDFTibK-Ek)*

### Locate the page(s) in the Pfizer C4591001 December 2020 interim report (or FDA briefing document) that show no demonstrated efficacy in participants over 75 years of age. Cotton says "it is written in the reports" — the FOIA'd / court-released Pfizer documents and the December 2020 FDA VRBPAC briefing should both contain this. Capture page numbers and add to the archive.
> ⏱ 04:55 · cited: Pfizer C4591001 interim clinical study report (Dec 2020)

**Summary:** The book directly and repeatedly documents the lack of demonstrated efficacy in people over 75 (citing Pfizer's December 10, 2020 FDA presentation by William Gruber and the OPECST report), making this one of its central claims.

**Where she discusses it in the book:**

- **Page 22** — Cotton's core statement that there is no statistical evidence of efficacy in over-75s, sourced to William Gruber's Dec 10, 2020 FDA presentation (footnote 30 links to fda.gov/media/144325/download). The accompanying table shows the >=75 years group with VE 100.0 and 95%CI (-13,1, 100,0).

> The reported efficacy is 100%, but the associated range is -13.1% to 100%. As a biostatistician, this indicates that there is no statistical evidence of vaccine efficacy in individuals over 75 years old. There were too few of them included at the time of the interim analysis to establish such evidence.

- **Page 113** — Documents that the official December 15, 2020 OPECST/Assemblee Nationale report ('The Covid-19 Vaccine Strategy', footnote 192) itself acknowledged efficacy could not be established for over-75s -- 'written in the reports' as Cotton says in the interview.

> On page 17, they once again confirmed one of my observations with the statement, “The efficacy could not be established for individuals over 75 years of age due to small sample sizes.” Consequently, this vulnerable population, often under treatment, was vaccinated without statistically demonstrated efficacy.

- **Page 22** — Quotes the February 2021 VIDAL website (footnote 32) confirming the data could not establish efficacy in over-75s or against severe forms, reinforcing that the limitation was on the public record.

> However, the results presented cannot confirm the vaccine’s efficacy against severe forms of COVID19, asymptomatic forms, or in individuals over 75 years old.

**Underlying sources she cites:**

| Footnote | Source document | Cited for | Link |
|---|---|---|---|
| 30 | FDA — William Gruber (Pfizer) VRBPAC presentation slides for BNT162b2, FDA meeting of 10 December 2020 (efficacy results by age group) | The 100% reported efficacy in individuals >=75 with a confidence interval of -13.1% to 100%, showing no statistical evidence of efficacy in those over 75 | [link](https://www.fda.gov/media/144325/download) |
| 32 | VIDAL (vidal.fr) — COMIRNATY vaccine page, February 2021 (Wayback Machine archive, captured 25 Feb 2021) | Statement that the presented results cannot confirm the vaccine's efficacy against severe/asymptomatic forms or in individuals over 75 years old | [link](https://web.archive.org/web/20210225221652/https://www.vidal.fr/maladies/voies-respiratoires/coronavirus-covid-19/vaccins.html) |
| 192 | French National Assembly / OPECST — information report 'The Covid-19 Vaccine Strategy' (l15b3695), 15 December 2020 | Page 17 statement that efficacy could not be established for individuals over 75 years of age due to small sample sizes | [link](https://www.assemblee-nationale.fr/dyn/15/rapports/ots/l15b3695_rapport-information.pdf) |

### Find the Moderna SEC filing (10-K or S-1) that classifies mRNA products as gene therapies. Cotton references it as the smoking-gun reclassification document.
> ⏱ 22:44 · cited: Moderna SEC 10-K / S-1 filing (gene-therapy classification)

**Summary:** The book quotes the Moderna SEC filing (December 2019 10-K) verbatim as its smoking-gun evidence that mRNA was classified by the FDA as a gene therapy product, exactly the document the follow-up seeks.

**Where she discusses it in the book:**

- **Page 190** — Introduces the Moderna SEC filing; footnote 295 links directly to sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1682852/.../moderna10-k12312019.htm -- the exact 10-K the todo asks to locate.

> The information given by Moderna to the US SECURITIES AND EXCHANGE COMMISSION as early as December 2019 should have alerted us.

- **Page 190** — Verbatim quote from the Moderna 10-K classifying mRNA as a gene therapy product -- the reclassification 'smoking gun' Cotton references in the interview.

> One can read: "Currently, mRNA is considered a gene therapy product by the FDA. In addition, because no product in which mRNA is the primary active ingredient has been approved, the regulatory pathway for approval is uncertain.

- **Page 190** — Cotton's interpretation of the SEC filing as evidence of deliberate non-classification to enable accelerated development -- the gene-therapy-vs-vaccine argument.

> The FDA has known all along that mRNA is a gene therapy, COVID vaccines were not classified as such to allow pharmaceutical companies to have an accelerated development for their vaccines, regulation far less restrictive than GT regulation which would have required more studies and more time !

**Underlying sources she cites:**

| Footnote | Source document | Cited for | Link |
|---|---|---|---|
| 295 | Moderna, Inc. — Annual Report Form 10-K for fiscal year ended 31 December 2019 (filed with U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, EDGAR) | Disclosure that 'Currently, mRNA is considered a gene therapy product by the FDA' and that the regulatory pathway is uncertain (the gene-therapy reclassification smoking gun) | [link](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1682852/000168285220000006/moderna10-k12312019.htm) · [archived](http://web.archive.org/web/20260326001610/https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1682852/000168285220000006/moderna10-k12312019.htm) |

### Locate the Pfizer C4591001 protocol section limiting Process 2 manufacturing validation to 250 patients per batch.
> ⏱ 13:31 · cited: Pfizer C4591001 Protocol (Process 2 amendment); Pfizer C4591001 trial database (FOIA / court-released)

**Summary:** The book documents the Process 1 vs Process 2 manufacturing change and states that the published protocol limited Process 2 validation to 250 participants (compared against 250 Process 1 vaccinees), the exact 'product administered is not the product tested' argument the todo concerns.

**Where she discusses it in the book:**

- **Page 191** — Directly states the C4591001 protocol limited Process 2 validation to 250 participants -- the specific protocol provision the todo asks to locate. Footnote 296 links to the NEJM protocol PDF.

> This change in manufacturing process was already mentioned into the published protocol. 250 participants were to receive vaccines from process 2. Immunogenicity (antibody assay) and safety of this process 2 were to be compared with a sample of 250 randomly selected vaccinated with process 1.

- **Page 192** — States the 'product administered is not the product tested' conclusion -- the marketed Process 2 product differed from the Process 1 trial material on which 95% efficacy was based.

> The public was led to believe they were receiving a vaccine with the 95% efficacy announced in December 2020, which was actually based on participants in the trial who had received process 1 or clinical trial material.

- **Page 191** — Documents the protocol amendment (20th amendment, Sept 2022) that removed the Process 1/Process 2 comparison objective, directly relevant to the 'Process 2 amendment' document cited in the todo.

> The latest protocol written by Pfizer in September 2022, 20th amendment removed this objective “as of July 3, 2022, over 3.6 billion doses of BNT162b2 have been distributed, with over 1.4 billion doses of BNT162b2 administered worldwide, which were manufactured via "Process 2". Given the number of doses now administered worldwide, the originally planned comparison of manufacturing processes is no longer warranted.”

**Underlying sources she cites:**

| Footnote | Source document | Cited for | Link |
|---|---|---|---|
| 296 | NEJM — Pfizer/BioNTech C4591001 clinical study protocol (supplementary appendix to Polack et al., NEJMoa2034577, December 2020) | The published protocol provision that 250 participants were to receive Process 2 vaccine, with immunogenicity and safety compared against 250 randomly selected Process 1 recipients | [link](https://www.nejm.org/doi/suppl/10.1056/NEJMoa2034577/suppl_file/nejmoa2034577_protocol.pdf) · [archived](http://web.archive.org/web/20260104135401/https://www.nejm.org/doi/suppl/10.1056/NEJMoa2034577/suppl_file/nejmoa2034577_protocol.pdf) |
| 299 | EMA — Comirnaty EPAR public assessment report (CHMP assessment report, 19 February 2021) | CHMP finding of two manufacturing processes (process 1 clinical trial material vs process 2 commercial) and the GMP/batch-release issues classified as a MAJOR OBJECTION | [link](https://www.ema.europa.eu/en/documents/assessment-report/comirnaty-epar-public-assessment-report_en.pdf) |
| 300 | BMJ — rapid response by Joshua Guetzkow on the Pfizer manufacturing-process change (bmj.o1731) | Independent academic work documenting the Process 1 / Process 2 manufacturing change | [link](https://www.bmj.com/content/378/bmj.o1731/rr-2) · [archived](http://web.archive.org/web/20260520074636/https://www.bmj.com/content/378/bmj.o1731/rr-2) |

### Find and link the European Parliament COVI hearing transcript or video where Pfizer's Janine Small confirmed that transmission was not tested before rollout (10 October 2022).
> ⏱ 01:56 · cited: European Parliament COVI committee hearing (10 Oct 2022) — Janine Small testimony

**Summary:** The book directly recounts the October 2022 European Parliament special COVI hearing where Janine Small (standing in for Albert Bourla) was forced to admit transmission was never studied, and provides the Parliament webstreaming URL in a footnote.

**Where she discusses it in the book:**

- **Page 158** — Identifies the same 10 Oct 2022 EP COVI hearing and Janine Small testimony; footnote 241 gives the Parliament's official webstreaming link (multimedia.europarl.europa.eu .../special-committee-on-COVID-19-pandemic_202210101430-COMMITTEE-COVI), which is exactly the source the follow-up asks to locate/link.

> Albert Bourla, who had been summoned to the European Parliament, did not deign to attend, leaving his place to Janine Small, in charge of the laboratory’s international markets. The hearing can still be viewed on the Parliament’s website241.

- **Page 159** — This is precisely the moment cited in the interview — Pfizer's Small publicly admitting transmission was not tested; footnote 242 also links Rob Roos's tweet of the exchange.

> Rob Roos, on the other hand, “forced” poor Janine Small, who was sent to the coalface by her director, to publicly admit that transmission had never been studied in the clinical trial, which was far from being a revelation for me as a biostatistician, since we had been aware of it since December 2020242 !!!!

- **Page 60** — Corroborates the substance of the transmission gap from Pfizer's own April 2021 documentation, showing Cotton knew transmission was never demonstrated long before Small's public admission.

> no evidence of efficacy against asymptomatic infection, against the long-term effects of COVID-19 disease, against the transmission of SARS-CoV-2, or against mortality

**Underlying sources she cites:**

| 242 | Tweet by MEP Rob Roos (X/Twitter), 11 October 2022, posting the clip in which Janine Small admits transmission was never studied in the trial | Rob Roos forcing Janine Small to publicly admit transmission had never been studied in the clinical trial | [link](https://twitter.com/Rob_Roos/status/1579759795225198593?s=20) |

### Add Augusto Roux's medical record (or a summary with source) to archive/sources/, as a permanent reference for the trial-injury argument.
> ⏱ 04:43 · cited: Augusto Roux medical file; Maddie de Garay testimony to Sen. Ron Johnson

**Summary:** The book devotes a full section to Augusto Roux's case for the trial-injury argument, quoting his EMA complaint email and his medical documents, though it does not reproduce the full medical record itself (which the follow-up asks to add to the archive).

**Where she discusses it in the book:**

- **Page 65** — Reproduces Roux's own account/complaint of his trial injuries — the core content of the medical record the follow-up wants archived; footnotes 85/86 (davidhealy.org/disappeared-in-argentina; vaccineharm.wordpress.com) point to where his documents were made public.

> "My name is Augusto German Roux, 36 years old, born on August 18, 1984, living in Buenos Aires, Argentina. I wanted to file a complaint regarding the adverse reactions I suffered after volunteering to receive the Pfizer SARSCov2 vaccine at the Cosme Argerich Central Military Hospital in the city of Buenos Aires. After the second dose, I had fever spikes of more than 40 degrees (which lasted for several weeks), I suffered from loss of consciousness, fainting, tachycardia that almost left me for dead, my urine was extremely dark, etc."

- **Page 66** — Documents the medical-record-vs-database discrepancy at the heart of the trial-injury argument; footnote 88 links a PubMed article (pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36710689) — a citable source for the estate request.

> Augusto and Brianne have published an article together, stating that four doctors examined Augusto and confirmed that his injuries were vaccine-induced88. Due to his health issues, Augusto discontinued his participation in the Pfizer clinical trial. However, the database records that he left the trial for personal reasons, not due to serious adverse events related to the vaccine.

- **Page 66** — Confirms Cotton's claim that she/Roux possess the underlying record evidence ("proof to the contrary"), matching the interview statement that she has the full record.

> The Argentinean center staff even added a non-existent suspected case of COVID-19 and an imaginary anxiety disorder to his record. Augusto has proof to the contrary.

**Underlying sources she cites:**

| Footnote | Source document | Cited for | Link |
|---|---|---|---|

| 86 | VaccineHarm (WordPress blog) — "Augusto German Roux" post, 29 December 2020 | Augusto Roux publicly revealing some of his medical documents to raise awareness of the vaccine's potential dangers | [link](https://vaccineharm.wordpress.com/2020/12/29/augusto-german-roux/) |
| 87 | YouTube video (Brianne Dressen testimony quoted in the book) | Brianne Dressen's account of her ordeal during the AstraZeneca clinical trial (told alongside Augusto Roux's case) | [link](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aDRTzUj1b58) |
| 88 | PubMed-indexed article co-authored by Augusto Roux and Brianne Dressen (PMID 36710689) | Article stating that four doctors examined Augusto and confirmed his injuries were vaccine-induced | [link](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36710689/) |

## Autorisation du vaccin COVID : erreurs ou complicités ?
<a id="snxhb5p5uty"></a>
*Virginie Joron · [watch ▶](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=snXhb5p5uTY) · [brief](./brief.html?id=snXhb5p5uTY)*

### Find the section of the Pfizer C4591001 protocol that authorizes antipyretic use during the trial, and the data table (interim or final CSR) confirming the 3.5× imbalance in antipyretic use between vaccine and placebo arms.
> ⏱ 02:36 · cited: Pfizer C4591001 interim clinical study report (Dec 2020) — antipyretic use tables

**Summary:** The book directly addresses the antipyretic confounding argument and states the vaccine group used far more antipyretics, biasing the 95% efficacy figure, though it does not state the precise 3.5x figure or cite a specific interim-CSR data table.

**Where she discusses it in the book:**

- **Page 95** — Establishes the mechanism Cotton describes in the interview: antipyretics suppress COVID symptoms, so fewer cases are tested/counted, inflating efficacy.

> Another problem is the permitted use of antipyretics, fever-reducing drugs, in case of a reaction right after the injections of each dose. These drugs may have actually suppressed fevers that are a symptom of COVID. You may have heard of people who were vaccinated and became ill within a few days of a dose. So using antipyretics means fewer cases of COVID in the trial.

- **Page 95** — Direct match to the interview claim that the vaccine arm used far more antipyretics than placebo, biasing the result; this is the imbalance the 3.5x figure refers to (exact ratio not given in book).

> Well, it is a big deal when you know that in the results, the vaccinated people used much more antipyretics than the saltwater vaccinated people did. This constitutes what we call a statistical bias, i.e., an element likely to make the results deviate from their true value.

- **Page 97** — Connects antipyretic use to the reactogenicity-diary data, the source from which the antipyretic-use imbalance in the trial is derived.

> Regarding the intake of antipyretics, it was possible to provide COVID cases for the subgroup of participants who had completed the reactogenicity diary.

### Locate and archive the FDA's June 2020 Guidance on Development and Licensure of Vaccines to Prevent COVID-19 — the document that allowed efficacy results based on just two months of follow-up. Cotton calls this the "freeway" the FDA built.
> ⏱ 11:06 · cited: FDA Guidance on Development and Licensure of Vaccines to Prevent COVID-19 (June 2020)

**Summary:** The book addresses the same regulatory pivot — an FDA rule that allowed COVID-vaccine authorizations on roughly two-to-three months of follow-up instead of the full two-year trial — but does not cite the June 2020 FDA guidance document by its title or date.

- **Page 181** — Directly describes the regulatory pivot the todo calls the FDA 'freeway' — a new FDA rule for COVID vaccines that legalized authorizations based on only ~3 months / two months of follow-up — though the book does not name the June 2020 'Guidance on Development and Licensure of Vaccines to Prevent COVID-19' by title.

> All the interim analyses upon which the marketing authorizations (issued in December 2020, April 2021, October 2021, and June 2022) were based, could only be conducted on participants followed up for a maximum of 3 months, thanks to a new regulation specific to anti-COVID vaccines hastily drafted by the FDA in response to the emergency. However, this very short follow-up time is entirely insufficient to evaluate the medium- and long-term safety, which remains unknown.

- **Page 181** — Explains the consequence of the FDA's shortened-follow-up rule — the same 'freeway' argument Cotton makes in the interview.

> It is worth noting that it is precisely this change in recommendations that conveniently allowed the laboratory to avoid presenting the antibody assay at 6 months after the second dose. Without this alteration, they would have been compelled to reconsider their revolutionary biotechnology, as no health agency would have been willing to grant authorization for a vaccine whose protection wanes in such a short period.

- **Page 14** — States the truncated-follow-up point (three months vs. the two-year trial) that the June 2020 FDA guidance enabled, matching Cotton's claim that efficacy was declared on far less than two years of data.

## Covid19 - Christine Cotton : \"La science ce n'est pas de la politique, c'est de la statistique !\"
<a id="prh1pkjrwhg"></a>
*Sud Radio · [watch ▶](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=prH1pkJrWhg) · [brief](./brief.html?id=prH1pkJrWhg)*

### Reproduce Cotton's 55% recalculation: pull the court-released Pfizer C4591001 trial database, run the analysis on the anti-nucleocapsid serology endpoint, and archive the script + result. This would give the archive an independently reproducible exhibit for the case-definition argument.
> ⏱ 16:25 · cited: Pfizer C4591001 trial database (FOIA / court-released)

**Summary:** The book directly addresses the recalculation: using Aaron Siri's publicly released data and the anti-nucleocapsid (non-S seroconversion) serology endpoint, Cotton recomputes vaccine efficacy at approximately 53% rather than 95% (the interview cites ~55%).

**Where she discusses it in the book:**

- **Page 98** — The core recalculation Cotton describes at ~16:25 — anti-nucleocapsid serology endpoint yields ~53% (interview: ~55%) instead of the 95% headline, driven by the choice of the PCR-confirmed primary endpoint.

> Upon recalculating based on the serological criterion using the data made publicly available by Mr. Siri, I’ve determined that the efficacy of the vaccine on ALL COVID cases, and not solely on mild or moderate symptomatic cases confirmed by PCR tests, is approximately 53%! This is significantly lower and, therefore, not as promising as the initially announced 95% efficacy.

- **Page 180** — Restates the recomputed efficacy figure and names the SAS database as the data source — the court-released Pfizer C4591001 dataset the todo asks to reproduce from.

> Furthermore, this criterion fails to represent the full spectrum of the disease, as efficacy calculated based on anti-nucleocapsid serology, also known as non-S-seroconversion to SARS-CoV-2 infection, which includes all COVID cases, is approximately 53%, calculated using the SAS® database.

- **Page 97** — Establishes that the anti-nucleocapsid serology endpoint underlying the recalculation existed in the trial but was withheld from the VRBPAC presentations — the case-definition argument.

> An anti-nucleocapsid serology, detecting antibodies produced in response to viral illness, was conducted at every planned visit, the results were never presented during each Vaccines and Related Biological Products Advisory Committee Meeting.

**Underlying sources she cites:**

**Summary:** The book addresses the Pfizer pregnancy trial (NCT04754594, started Feb 2021) in detail, noting Pfizer's prolonged delay and incomplete results; it nuances the interview claim, stating results were eventually published (July 2023 and finally July 2024) but were too incomplete to conclude on efficacy in pregnant women.

In February 2022, Pfizer revealed that they still did not have "a complete set of data." It was not until July 13, 2023, that Pfizer published the results and the study documents.

- **Page 166** — Directly supports and refines Cotton's interview statement that results were not released — the book says they were delayed/incomplete and only fully published in July 2024, with no usable efficacy conclusion in pregnant women.

The number of COVID cases and the results of neutralizing antibody titers were missing. Pfizer has therefore taken a year to publish results that do not allow for any conclusions regarding the vaccine’s efficacy in pregnant women and present erroneous tolerance results. The missing results were finally published in July 2024.

The authors found the spike protein in the placental tissue, demonstrating that the COVID-19 mRNA vaccine is capable of penetrating the fetal-placental barrier and reaching the intrauterine environment

- **Page 73** — Documents biodistribution beyond the injection site (ovaries, organs), the mechanistic basis for the placental-transfer / pregnancy-safety concern.

> The manufacturer’s pharmacokinetic studies show that the Lipid Nano Particles (LNP) are distributed mainly in the muscle at the injection site and in the liver. According to a confidential Pfizer document obtained through the FOIA, the LNP were found in the ovaries of female rats101. Subsequent publications have shown that they transport mRNA throughout the body and are found in the heart, brain, and other organs

- **Page 166** — Exposes the gap between official pregnancy recommendations and the absence of adequate pregnancy-safety data, which the todo asks to cross-link.

> Finally, despite the one-year delay between the end of the trial and the publication of results on the clinical trial website, the results submitted in October 2023, which we have just reviewed, are very incomplete... Pfizer has therefore taken a year to publish results that do not allow for any conclusions regarding the vaccine’s efficacy in pregnant women and present erroneous tolerance results.

**Summary:** The book directly addresses pre-vaccination institutional knowledge of severe UK allergic reactions and the adrenaline-auto-injector warning, though it cites the UK MHRA's 9 December 2020 alert rather than the December 2020 OPECST report named in the todo.

**Where she discusses it in the book:**

> As early as December 9, 2020, the British regulatory agency, the MHRA, issued an alert: “Anyone with a history of significant allergic reaction to a vaccine, drug, or food (such as a history of anaphylactoid reaction or those advised to carry an adrenaline auto-injector) should not receive the Pfizer BioNTech vaccine” the regulatory agency said.

- **Page 37** — Confirms that severe allergic reaction (anaphylaxis) was a formally recognized risk in the December 2020 EMA/Pfizer Risk Management Plan, reinforcing that the allergic-reaction danger was known institutionally before mass vaccination.

> Anaphylaxis was classified as an important identified risk in December 2020.

### Find the EPI-PHARE / Epifar study that, per Cotton, defines an "adverse event" only within 21 days of injection — useful as an example of how real-world studies under-detect long-tail harms.
> ⏱ 10:33 · cited: Pfizer C4591001 interim clinical study report (Dec 2020) — antipyretic use tables

**Summary:** The book does not name the EPI-PHARE / Epifar study, but it directly makes the same methodological criticism — that limiting the counting of adverse events to within 21 days of injection systematically under-detects long-tail harms — using a CDC June 2021 table as its example.

**Where she discusses it in the book:**

- **Page 124** — Concrete example of the 21-day adverse-event window the todo describes, used by Cotton to illustrate how real-world/regulatory analyses define an 'adverse event' only within 21 days of injection.

- **Page 125** — States Cotton's exact methodological objection — the same point she makes in the interview about real-world studies under-detecting long-tail harms by capping the window at 21 days.

> Is it reasonable to limit counting adverse events to within 21 days after a single injection? Considering the time required to establish a diagnosis, to have an autopsy result, it would have been more prudent to adjust the usual standards to ensure that no serious signals were overlooked, wouldn’t it?

- **Page 91** — Supports the broader argument that real-world/retrospective studies (the category EPI-PHARE/Epifar belongs to) are methodologically weak and systematically conclude there is no problem; the specific EPI-PHARE/Epifar study itself is not named in the book.

> Even worse, so-called retrospective studies conducted on existing databases have the lowest level of evidence among all study types, indicating that their results should be approached with caution.

## CeNC - Commission d'enquête nationale citoyenne - bio-statisticienne Christine Cotton témoigne
<a id="d69q58e0rmm"></a>
*Actualité Politique Du Québec · [watch ▶](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=D69Q58E0rMM) · [brief](./brief.html?id=D69Q58E0rMM)*

### Locate the PCR-testing-rate comparison in the court-released SAS tables (vaccine arm vs. placebo arm) that supports Cotton's "fewer tests in vaccine arm" claim. Capture table name and the difference in tests-per-suspected-case.
> ⏱ 09:43 · cited: Pfizer C4591001 SAS datasets (court-released)

**Summary:** The book directly supports Cotton's case-detection-bias argument: it confirms in the database that vaccine-arm participants were tested less often than placebo participants, ties this and antipyretic use to undercounted COVID cases, and recalculates efficacy at ~53% on the serological criterion (vs. the 95% PCR endpoint).

**Where she discusses it in the book:**

- **Page 99** — This is the book's explicit version of the interview claim that the vaccine arm had fewer tests; footnote 150 cites the OpenVAET C4591001 trial audit (openvaet.substack.com) drawn from the released data, the closest the book comes to naming the underlying SAS-table comparison.

> Moreover, we have confirmation in the database that participants who received the vaccine were tested less frequently than those in the placebo group, which introduced a major bias in counting the number of COVID-19 cases, the primary efficacy criterion, in favor of the vaccine150.

- **Page 99** — Matches the pull-quote's anti-N serology recalculation (interview said ~55%; book says ~53%) showing efficacy collapses from 95% when all COVID cases are counted, and credits the court-released data ('Mr. Siri').

> Upon recalculating based on the serological criterion using the data made publicly available by Mr. Siri, I’ve determined that the efficacy of the vaccine on ALL COVID cases, and not solely on mild or moderate symptomatic cases confirmed by PCR tests, is approximately 53%!

- **Page 96** — Supports the antipyretic limb of the case-detection-bias argument from the pull quote — vaccine-arm participants took fever-reducers more often, masking COVID symptoms and undercounting cases.

> Well, it is a big deal when you know that in the results, the vaccinated people used much more antipyretics than the saltwater vaccinated people did. This constitutes what we call a statistical bias, i.e., an element likely to make the results deviate from their true value.

**Underlying sources she cites:**

| Footnote | Source document | Cited for | Link |
|---|---|---|---|

| — (named in text) | Pfizer C4591001 trial data made publicly available via Aaron Siri (the court-released SAS datasets; the data Cotton recalculates from) | Cotton's recalculation on the serological (anti-nucleocapsid) criterion yielding ~53% efficacy on ALL COVID cases rather than the announced 95% | — |

- **Page 100** — Describes the Ventavia data-integrity violations alleged by Jackson and two anonymous co-whistleblowers.

> Brook, along with two other employees who wished to remain anonymous, have accused Ventavia of several infractions, including falsifying data, employing inadequately trained staff, delaying follow-up on reported adverse events, failing to complete data entry promptly, and neglecting to conduct PCR testing on all participants with COVID-19-like symptoms to confirm the presence or absence of the disease.

- **Page 101** — Confirms the three trial centers detail and the larger audit implication that exemplifies Cotton's call for an independent multi-center audit.

> The Ventavia case pertains to only three sites, encompassing a little over 1000 participants, yet this raises concerns about the management of all other sites.

- **unknown** — Provides litigation status (ongoing suit vs Ventavia and Pfizer) and a case-documents source; the BMJ Thacker article is cited separately at footnote 153 (bmj.com/content/375/bmj.n2635).

> Since January 2021, Brook’s legal battle against Ventavia and Pfizer has been raging. All documents in the case are openly available for the world to follow the progress of the case

## Documents déclassifiés de Pfizer et revelations choc de Christine Cotton
<a id="x8arx5y"></a>
*Sud Radio · [watch ▶](https://www.dailymotion.com/video/x8arx5y) · [brief](./brief.html?id=x8arx5y)*

### Track the "Jikkyleaks" thread on the 4444 patient IDs and the Argentine military-hospital recruitment ramp — capture the underlying SAS table names and the screenshots/code Jikky used so the analysis is reproducible from the archive.
> ⏱ 09:33 · cited: Pfizer C4591001 SAS datasets (court-released); CDISC standards

**Summary:** The book does not name 'Jikkyleaks' or the '4444 patient IDs' specifically, but it directly and substantially addresses the same underlying data-integrity topic: the court-released SAS/XPT datasets, their CDISC non-compliance, and the anomalous recruitment ramp at the Argentine Cosme Argerich Central Military Hospital center.

**Where she discusses it in the book:**

- **Page 66** — Names the court-released SAS XPT datasets and the CDISC standards the follow-up asks about, and documents that Pfizer's SAS tables omitted variables — the reproducibility/data-integrity concern Jikky's thread also pursued.

> The XPT files, or "transport" files, are generated by the SAS® software used to program the analyses of the Pfizer/BioNtech clinical trial and originate from the laboratory itself. Any SAS® user can import them to convert them back into SAS® tables and check the results included in the clinical reports. The format of these tables, which constitute the database, is governed by pharmaceutical industry standards. They must comply with the structure established by CDISC (Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium). It is worth noting that the SAS® tables provided by Pfizer do not follow these recommendations; there are missing variables in the datasets, seemingly intended to complicate the calculations for those competent enough to reprogram them.

- **Page 66** — Quantifies the Argentine military-hospital recruitment ramp (the 'recruitment ramp' the follow-up flags), showing the center's outsized enrollment versus the whole trial.

> The Argentinean site that recruited Augusto Roux included more than 5700 participants between April 2020 and November 2020, and this out of a total of 40,000. 10,000 participants were recruited across only 5 centers.

- **Page 66** — Describes the collaborative whistleblower analysis of the court-released site-level data — the milieu in which Jikky's 4444-patient-ID thread arose — even though Jikky and the specific table names are not cited.

> Thanks to documents made public by a court decision, several whistleblowers have been examining the files containing data reported by each participant's site for over a year now.

**Underlying sources she cites:**

| Footnote | Source document | Cited for | Link |
|---|---|---|---|
| 85 | DavidHealy.org blog — "Disappeared in Argentina" (account of Augusto Roux, the Argentine C4591001 trial participant whose serious adverse event was reclassified in the database) | The Argentine-center data-integrity case: Augusto Roux's adverse events being misrecorded as a personal-reason withdrawal, the context for re-examining the released SAS/XPT files. | [link](https://davidhealy.org/disappeared-in-argentina/) |
| 86 | VaccineHarm (WordPress) — "Augusto German Roux" post, 29 Dec 2020 (Roux's public disclosure of his medical documents from the Argentine Pfizer trial site) | Augusto Roux publicly revealing his medical documents to expose data manipulation at the Argentine recruiting center. | [link](https://vaccineharm.wordpress.com/2020/12/29/augusto-german-roux/) |
| 88 | PubMed (NCBI) — peer-reviewed article co-authored by Augusto Roux and Brianne Dressen documenting that four doctors confirmed his injuries were vaccine-induced (PMID 36710689) | Independent medical confirmation of Roux's vaccine-induced injuries, contradicting the trial database's reclassification of his withdrawal. | [link](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36710689/) |

> In France, as soon as May 2022, several victims have already filed a complaint against "unknown parties" for poisoning. Many legal actions are ongoing against Pfizer but obtaining formal recognition of a link between vaccination and death is a real assault course.
